response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with strong TDD and craftsmanship credentials, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with a Principal Performance Architect role focused on SoC/IP modeling and AI accelerator optimization. He has no demonstrated experience in hardware architecture, GPU/SIMD/MIMD systems, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. His closest relevant work is low-latency DLang services and some embedded exposure, which falls well short of the principal-level hardware performance expertise required.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec in DLang with async I/O)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/mentoring
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or accelerator performance background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture at the hardware level

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or DL workload optimization background
Low-latency systems and performance-oriented D programming
